notes: Lovers finale cards. Each is a way the afternoon didn't land. The arcana's distinctive failure is RIFT — Reed leaves with the boxes but you didn't manage to be in the room together. The diner is two floors below all of these endings, oblivious.

finales.title: THE RIFT
finales.flavor: Reed picks up the last box. They were here for two hours and forty-three minutes. SYNC never came up. The painting on the easel is still two-thirds done, and Reed glances at it on the way out and decides, in that glance, to leave it. Sasha is at the front door. Reed is at the fire escape. They go down opposite directions of the building — Sasha back into the apartment, Reed down into the alley. The diner sounds come up through the floor, oblivious. The record on the turntable finished an hour ago. Neither of them changed it. The needle is sitting in the silent groove at the inner edge of the side.

finales.title: THE SILENT APARTMENT
finales.flavor: Sasha can't talk to them. They want to. They have been planning what to say since Tuesday. Tonight is Saturday and the sentences they planned have all gone stiff in their mouth. Sasha sits on the bed corner. Reed sits at the easel. The record skips into silence and stays there. Neither of them changes it. The afternoon goes quiet and stays quiet. The radiator clicks; the diner bell rings; somebody on the street below laughs. Inside the apartment is one of those silences that becomes a thing in the room — that takes up a chair at the kitchen counter, that picks up the kettle, that you have to set a place for.

finales.title: LEFT WITH THE BOXES
finales.flavor: The neighbor left without saying anything. The bus kid never came up; she had a tray of plates downstairs that needed her. The old friend stayed in the doorway and called something out that nobody quite heard. Reed stacked the last boxes alone — six boxes, the tape on three of them re-applied because the tape they brought wasn't sticking in the cold. Sasha was somewhere else in the apartment for most of it. Not far. Just on the other side of the easel, looking at the painting and not finishing it. Reed leaves quietly. The downstairs door closes behind them without slamming.

finales.title: THE AFTERNOON RAN OUT
finales.flavor: It's six. The diner downstairs is busy — the evening rush has started; you can hear John's call bell every minute or so. Reed has the boxes by the door. They didn't get to the conversation that was supposed to be the conversation. They will get to it. They will both get to it. The afternoon was just the afternoon and the conversation is its own thing, and the conversation will pick a different room — a different afternoon, a year from now, in the apartment when the painting is finally finished, with Sasha moving out and Reed coming over because Sasha called. The conversation is not gone. It just isn't tonight.

post_loss_behavior.note: Lovers losses leave a little residual SYNC carrying forward. The next afternoon starts with you having already crossed the room once. The room remembers.
